tldr: The hackathon-cloud conversion is viable, but the existing demo-mode bypass, single-owner authorization model, and new GCP signed-URL design must be resolved before implementation.
decision_reason: Minimum confidence is 4 (< 8), every reviewer marked high risk, and security blocked the plan because the current demo fallback can grant unauthenticated owner access and the migration changes the authorization boundary.

summary_by_agent.eng: The existing release primitives are reusable, but the migration is cross-cutting and needs a distinct HACKATHON mode plus a concrete GCS signing design.
summary_by_agent.design: Public onboarding, organization/team binding, quota states, and schedule removal need an explicit multi-page journey rather than minor connection-page edits.
summary_by_agent.qa: Existing unit tests are strong for the private MVP but do not cover multi-user authorization, GCP federation, quotas, or real database migrations.
summary_by_agent.security: Block pending a fail-closed deployment mode, centralized server-side membership authorization, secure admission-code handling, and exclusive external-account claims.

key_findings: Introduce an explicit HACKATHON deployment mode; production must reject demo behavior rather than treating a missing DEMO_MODE as a demo owner.
Replace User.workspaceId/OwnerContext authorization atomically with server-side membership lookup and explicit OWNER/MEMBER policy before enabling public sign-in.
Use a database-backed, high-entropy, HMAC/pepper-protected registration admission record with atomic consumption and durable throttling.
Keep Better Auth GitHub sign-in separate from GitHub App installation; do not retain unnecessary social OAuth tokens in plaintext account columns.
Bind and exclusively claim GitHub organization installations and Vercel teams per workspace; preserve release-time snapshot and ownership validation.
Design GCS artifact URLs around a restricted signer service account using IAM signBlob or a bounded proxy; Vercel OIDC alone cannot sign a GCS V4 URL.
Use GCS generation preconditions, canonical workspace/kind/hash validation, private uniform bucket access, strict upload bounds, and fail-closed KMS/storage errors.
Disable schedules in the API and cron execution path for Hobby mode, not only in the UI; add transactional server-side quotas and real PostgreSQL/two-tenant tests.
human_in_loop_reason_if_applicable: Authentication, authorization, cryptography, and infrastructure changes require explicit approval of the revised security design before code changes.
